subject: Product Management
title: The S-Curve of Growth
description: A short lesson to help you apply The S-Curve of Growth.

hook.text: Every product follows an S-curve: slow start, explosive growth, then plateau. The question is when each phase begins.

buildup.text: The slow start is product-market fit. Explosive growth is the network effects or viral loop kicking in.

discovery.text: Companies that recognize the plateau early can jump to the next S-curve — a new market, product, or business model.

twist.text: The most dangerous moment is mid-growth: everything seems fine, so nobody prepares for the inevitable slowdown ahead.

climax.text: Amazon jumped S-curves from bookstore to everything store to cloud computing to AI. Each new curve extended the growth.

punchline.text: Ride the curve. But start building the next one before this one peaks.

quiz.question: What should companies do when they recognize a growth plateau approaching?
options: Start building the next S-curve — a new market, product, or business model
Continue investing only in the current product
Accept that growth is permanently over
